title: Filipino Sirloin Steak Rolls
description: A unique fusion of Filipino and steakhouse flavors, these rolls are perfect for a special occasion or dinner party.
instructions: 1. Preheat a non-stick skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat.
2. Season the sirloin steak with black pepper.
3. Cook the steak for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
4. Let the steak rest for 5 minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
5. In a small bowl, mix together the savory soy sauce and lime juice.
6. Brush the mixture onto the sliced steak.
7. Heat about 1-2 inches of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
8. Once the oil is hot, add a few of the spring roll wrappers to the oil and fry until they are crispy and golden brown.
9. Remove the fried wrappers from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
10. To assemble the rolls, place a few slices of the steak onto a fried wrapper, followed by a spoonful of pineapple salsa and mango chutney.
11. Fold the bottom half of the wrapper up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrapper up tightly.
12. Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve the rolls hot.
